page: Amazon Web Services x8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It has no local storage or GPUs, and relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the x8i.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. It achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core tests, image processing, and text processing. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers a cached read-only bandwidth of 3831.46 GB/sec. For database workloads, the instance handles 90.13 million Redis SET operations per second and shows strong Passmark database performance. It also supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling efficient low-precision matrix operations for AI workloads, as evidenced by strong prompt processing speeds on large language models.

Due to its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16:1, this instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications, large-scale databases, and in-memory caching layers. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires network-attached storage configurations, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, real-time analytics, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features a 3.9 GHz clock speed and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. It delivers top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth of 3831.46 GB/sec for cached blocks. With hardware-accelerated support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and text processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large databases and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, built for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16 GB per core
Top-tier performance in single-core execution and image processing
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix operations
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
